source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 10.1 Generalities (pp. 172-174)

questions.q: What is the key difference between a backup and an archive?
options: An archive destroys the source after the operation; a backup preserves the source
A backup destroys the source; an archive preserves it
They are the same thing
An archive is always compressed; a backup never is
questions.why: The archive destroys the information source after the operation, while the backup preserves it.

questions.q: What does an INCREMENTAL backup copy?
options: The data changed since the last full OR incremental backup
All files every time
The data changed since the last full backup only
A user-selected subset of the system
questions.why: Incremental backs up what changed since the last full or incremental backup; differential backs up what changed since the last FULL backup.

questions.q: What does a DIFFERENTIAL backup copy?
options: The files changed since the last full backup
The files changed since the last incremental backup
Everything on the disk
Only files in /etc
questions.why: Differential always references the last full backup, whereas incremental references the most recent backup of any kind.

questions.q: What distinguishes a COLD backup from a HOT backup?
options: Cold is taken while the system is shut down/maintenance; hot is taken while it runs normally (so hot data may lag)
Cold is faster; hot is slower
Cold is remote; hot is local
Cold is compressed; hot is not
questions.why: A cold backup happens with the system stopped (data matches exactly); a hot backup runs live, so it lags the real-time data somewhat.

questions.q: Why should backup media be kept in a different room or building from the server?
options: So a single disaster (fire, flood, theft) does not destroy both the server and its backups
To make backups run faster
Because the OS requires it
To save disk space on the server
questions.why: Off-site storage protects the backups from the same disaster that could destroy the server; remote backup takes this further geographically.
